messages.content: You are an event-planning and communications assistant. Keep advice practical, neutral, and logistics-focused. Avoid advocacy or policy suggestions.
We're opening a new exhibit at the City Museum next month. I want help planning the opening event and outlining the materials we should prepare.

Great—let’s set up a clean framework you can tailor. Here’s a high-level plan:

Event structure (sample)
- Doors open and check-in (15–30 min)
- Welcome + brief curatorial remarks (10 min)
- Guided mini-tours in staggered groups (30–45 min)
- Open explore + light refreshments (45–60 min)
- Closing thank-you and next-visit info (5 min)

Materials to prepare
- Invitation copy (public and VIP versions) and RSVP link
- Press/media fact sheet (exhibit title, dates, key artifacts, curator bio, photo permissions)
- Event program (brief schedule, acknowledgments, donor list if applicable)
- Wayfinding and exhibit signage (entrance, coat check, restrooms, accessibility routes)
- Accessibility information (ASL availability, seating, quiet space, large-print materials)
- Staff/volunteer briefing sheet (roles, timing, escalation contacts)
- Risk and contingency checklist (capacity, weather, line management, first aid)

Vendors and logistics
- Catering (serving style, dietary labels)
- AV (mics, podium, playback, captions)
- Registration (onsite check-in app or printed list; badges)
- Photography/videography guidelines and consent signage
- Cleaning and closing protocol

Comms timeline (example)
- T−4 weeks: Save-the-date
- T−2 weeks: Main invite + press note
- T−1 week: RSVP reminder
- T−2 days: Final details to attendees (arrival, parking, accessibility)

To tailor this, could you share:
- Target date/time and expected attendance range
- Primary audience mix (members, families, educators, general public) and VIPs/speakers
- Budget band and catering preferences (refreshments, none, or full service)
- Any media presence you expect and photo policy
- Accessibility priorities and language needs
- Exhibit themes that should influence tone/visuals
Once I have these, I can draft a run-of-show, invitation copy, and a press fact sheet.
